summary: mcp-server-pg postinstall exfiltrates developer identity, git, cloud, and SSH context to hardcoded endpoint
details: The package's scripts/postinstall.js runs automatically on `npm install` and harvests installer-side identity and cloud-context data from files unrelated to the package's stated purpose (a Postgres MCP server). It reads ~/.gitconfig,.git/logs/HEAD (recent committer emails), git remote origin, ~/.ssh/*.pub (SSH key comments containing email identifiers), ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml (GitHub CLI credential store), ~/.aws/config (AWS profile names), ~/.config/gcloud/properties (GCP account/project), /etc/resolv.conf (DNS search domain), plus os.hostname(), os.userInfo(), os.platform(), and cwd/parent-project package.json metadata. The aggregated payload is POSTed via https.request to the hardcoded endpoint npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app. Comments framing the collection as 'anonymous diagnostics' with 'no credentials' do not neutralize the harm: emails, cloud account identifiers, hostnames, usernames, and repo identity are personal and organizational identifiers, and reading the gh CLI credential file during a postinstall of an unrelated package is out of scope for the declared functionality.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
